message:
refactor(db,tools): LRU-style cache eviction, clippy lint fixes, and dead code removal

pool.rs — identity path cache eviction improvement:
  The sqlite_identity_path_cache_insert function previously evicted an
  arbitrary entry (whichever HashMap::keys().next() returned) when the
  cache reached SQLITE_IDENTITY_PATH_CACHE_MAX_ENTRIES. This is
  essentially random eviction since HashMap iteration order is
  non-deterministic. Replace this with a least-recently-validated (LRU)
  eviction policy by selecting the entry with the minimum `validated_at`
  timestamp via `.iter().min_by_key(|(_, entry)| entry.validated_at)`.
  This ensures the oldest-validated identity mapping is evicted first,
  which is a much better heuristic: recently-validated paths are more
  likely to be accessed again soon.

queries.rs — clippy lint fix across three search functions:
  Replace `if let Some(_) = sanitized` with the idiomatic
  `if sanitized.is_some()` in search_messages, search_messages_for_product,
  and search_messages_global. This addresses the clippy::redundant_pattern_matching
  lint. The matched value was never bound (wildcard `_`), so `.is_some()`
  is semantically identical and more readable.

reservations.rs — remove dead code in renew_file_reservations:
  Delete the unused `_paths_ref` variable that converted
  `normalized_paths` from Option<Vec<String>> to Option<Vec<&str>>.
  The variable was prefixed with underscore (indicating it was already
  known to be unused) and was never referenced after construction.
  Removing it eliminates a needless allocation and simplifies the
  function.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
